channel.title: DBMS Digest
channel.description: A weekly, ad-free, fact-checked roundup of PostgreSQL & wider DBMS internals, news, and research. Monday → Sunday.

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-14 (week of 2026-06-08–2026-06-14)
item.content:encoded:
PostgreSQL

  • How the Other Half Counts — Christophe Pettus surveys how Oracle, Db2, MySQL, SQLite, DuckDB, and Snowflake gather planner statistics vs. PostgreSQL's sampling ANALYZE; the real axis turns out to be stats freshness and who owns it, not the join algorithm. (thebuild.com)
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: deadlock_timeout — clarifies that deadlock_timeout sets how often PostgreSQL checks for a deadlock, not how long a deadlock is tolerated; part of a near-daily GUC-internals series. (Christophe Pettus · thebuild.com)
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: DateStyle — on why DateStyle conflates two unrelated behaviours (input parsing and output formatting) in a single GUC. (Christophe Pettus · thebuild.com)
  • All Your GUCs in a Row: data_sync_retry — the GUC born from the Postgres fsync-reliability durability saga, and why the default is what it is. (Christophe Pettus · thebuild.com)
  • My Three Top PostgreSQL 19 Features — Stefanie Janine walks through INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO SELECT, the new in-core REPACK (with CONCURRENTLY), and SQL/PGQ graph queries landing in PG19. (proopensource.it)
  • PostgreSQL Anonymizer 3.1: local differential privacy — Dalibo's data-masking extension adds local-differential-privacy masking techniques (announcement light on detail; verify changelog before relying on it). (Dalibo · postgresql.org)

PostgreSQL mailing lists

  • [hackers] FK fast-path: OOB write + missed FK checks during batched RI checks — three defects in PG19's new batched foreign-key fast path (an out-of-bounds write and two paths silently committing FK-violating orphan rows); reproducers confirmed, the fix is pushed and on the PG19 Open Items list. Data-integrity regression in a just-shipped beta feature. (Amit Langote · pgsql-hackers) [committed]
  • [hackers] Commit Sequence Numbers and Visibility (CSN snapshots) — Jeff Davis proposes concrete rules for CSN-based snapshot acquisition; Matthias van de Meent pushes back that coupling snapshot acquisition to durability would impose synchronous-flush waits on readers, counter-proposing separate durability-horizon WAL records. Core architectural debate for a future MVCC redesign. (Jeff Davis · pgsql-hackers) [open]
  • [hackers] RFC: a constraint-enforcement layer for cross-partition uniqueness — Greg Burd reframes the perennial "global unique index on partitioned tables" problem as a separate pg_constraint_method abstraction (a lightweight WAL-logged membership map) instead of a full global index, citing prior global-index attempts. (Greg Burd · pgsql-hackers) [open]
  • [hackers] Any JIT-compiled query crashes (SIGILL) on libLLVM 19 + ASAN CI build — cfbot failures blamed on the Row Pattern Recognition patch turn out to be a feature-independent crash: on the ASAN+LLVM 19.1 config any JIT-compiled query SIGILLs on plain master; minimal repro and regression test posted. Was masking real CI signal on in-flight patches. (pgsql-hackers) [patch posted]

Community pulse

  • PgDog is funded and coming to a database near you — a funding post for a Rust connection-pooler/sharder for Postgres became the week's biggest DB thread; the substance is the 261-comment debate over whether sharding belongs in a proxy and how it compares to PgBouncer / PgCat / Citus. (Hacker News · 548 pts, 261 comments)
  • Show HN: HelixDB — a graph database built on object storage — an OLTP graph DB on S3 with native vector + FTS drew a long back-and-forth on the latency trade-offs of running transactions against cold object storage. (Hacker News · 158 pts, 46 comments)
  • Spanish traders set the standard for GnuCash database design — an essay on how centuries-old double-entry bookkeeping prefigured GnuCash's schema turned into an 82-comment thread on modelling money in a relational database. (Hacker News · 133 pts, 82 comments)

Wider DBMS & distributed data

  • The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE — argues that under MVCC, bulk DELETE doesn't cheaply reclaim space (dead tuples, VACUUM, bloat), so partition-drop / DROP TABLE is the only delete that scales; lively HN discussion. (planetscale.com)
  • Our MongoDB TLA+ workshop — Murat Demirbas reports an order-of-magnitude better onboarding when LLM assistance removes TLA+ syntax as the barrier, letting engineers formally model real replication/storage subsystems hands-on; includes a distilled "AI-assisted TLA+ best practices" guide. (muratbuffalo.blogspot.com)
  • Postgres by Example — example-driven Postgres reference repo that surfaced on HN; useful as a teaching/cheat-sheet resource. (github.com)

Commercial engines (SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, …)

  • RUM: storing more in the index — Franck Pachot deep-dive on Postgres RUM indexes (carrying extra payload in the index to serve ESR-style equality/sort/range queries the way other engines' covering indexes do). (dev.to)
  • GIN: understanding PostgreSQL's inverted index and its limitations — Pachot walks the internals and trade-offs of GIN indexing, with cross-engine framing. (dev.to)

International (non-English sources)

  • Postgres Pro evolves a "Virtual DBA" LLM agent's environment with record/replay — Postgres Professional ran an evolutionary search over a Postgres-diagnosis agent's setup (prompts, analysis steps, MCP tools) by recording and fast-replaying real workloads; a headline finding is that handing the agent more tools can actually worsen its final verdict. (Postgres Professional · habr.com) [ru] (orig: «Промпты, RAG, LLM-тюнинг, Harness… Идём дальше?»)
  • Three discovery problems with Postgres master/replica — and a lightweight fix — finding the master after failover, picking a replica within an acceptable lag, and guaranteeing read-your-writes, solved with a small HTTP service (pg-status) instead of DNS/HAProxy hacks that don't understand replication LSN. (krylosov-aa · habr.com) [ru] (orig: «Три задачи discovery при работе с PostgreSQL master/replica — и как их решить»)
  • OceanBase lands a batch of papers at SIGMOD/VLDB 2026 — surfaced via the Chinese DB community 墨天轮; the Ant Group distributed-RDBMS vendor's top-venue haul, with three worth mining for our own engine: TaCo (query-aware subspace collision for high-dimensional ANN search), ESTune (Bayesian uncertainty-guided early stopping for knob tuning), and Secure Multi-Party Sampling over Joins (VLDB). (墨天轮 / modb.pro) [zh] (orig: «OceanBase 多篇论文被国际顶会录用»)

Research & cutting edge

  • Multiversion Concurrency Control for Multiversion B-Trees — cMVBT redesigns the multiversion B-tree with optimistic latches for writers and latch-free range scans plus continuous GC, beating version-chain MVCC on write throughput and arbitrary-version range scans. (arXiv) [paper]
  • Bespoke-Card: synthesizing workload-specific cardinality estimators — generate a per-workload cardinality estimator instead of tuning one generic model. (AIDB @ VLDB '26) [paper]
  • Determination Provenance: From Ambiguity to Algebra — Joseph M. Hellerstein gives an algebraic treatment of determination/provenance. (arXiv) [paper]

Call for papers

Forward-looking; open as of 2026-06-20, nearest deadline first.

  • PGDay Israel 2026 — CFP closes Jul 1, 2026. (community)
  • VLDB 2027 — Athens, Aug 23–27, 2027 — rolling monthly PVLDB research deadlines; next on/around Jul 1, 2026. (research)
  • SIGMOD 2027 — Huntington Beach, Jun 13–19, 2027 — research-track Round 3 closes Jul 17, 2026. (research)
  • CIDR 2027 — Amsterdam, Jan 24–27, 2027 — CFP open; deadline on the page. (research)

(PGConf.EU 2026's CFP closed Jun 1, so it's excluded — the section lists only still-open calls.)

Upcoming events

Next ~4–6 weeks. Talk picks flagged for an internals developer — ideas worth probing for our own products.

  • Swiss PGDay 2026 — Rapperswil, CH, Jun 25–26 — the Swiss PostgreSQL conference, two tracks (EN/DE). Internals picks:
    • Deep dive into the PostgreSQL Front/Backend protocol (Xavier Fischer, EDB) — the wire protocol every pooler, proxy, and distributed router must speak; directly useful for anything that intercepts or multiplexes client↔server traffic.
    • Optimizer Hints in PostgreSQL (Michael Banck, credativ) — timely against PG19's new pg_plan_advice; where hint-style plan control is heading and how to expose it.
    • Zooming in on OOM: a deep dive into Postgres and Linux memory (Oleksii Kliukin & Dimitris Sideris, Tiger Data) — how backends interact with the Linux OOM killer; relevant to memory accounting and stability under load.

July itself is quiet for relational-DB conferences (only a Postgres meetup in Istanbul on Jul 2, programme TBA); the next program-rich events are PGDay UK (Sep 8) and PGConf.EU (Valencia, Oct 20–23).

23 items · sources scanned: ~30 · community threads scanned: HN (40 DB stories) · international: 3 (ru, zh) · CFPs open: 4 · upcoming events: 1 · filtered out as marketing/ads: 8

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-06-07 (week of 2026-06-01–2026-06-07)
item.content:encoded:
PostgreSQL

  • PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1 released — first beta of PG19 lands with a big feature set: in-core REPACK [CONCURRENTLY], the pg_plan_advice/pg_stash_advice planner-stabilization extensions ("plan advice", not "hints"), parallel autovacuum, online enable/disable of data checksums and logical replication without restart (effective_wal_level), SQL/PGQ property-graph queries, WAIT FOR LSN, INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO SELECT, eager aggregation, JIT now off by default, and default_toast_compression=lz4. (postgresql.org)
  • Postgres 19 gets "plan advice" (query hints, sort of) — a walk through the new pg_plan_advice / pg_stash_advice contrib modules that let you capture and reapply planner decisions by query id, ending the project's long-standing "no hints" posture. (Shaun Thomas · pgEdge)
  • Your first graph query in PostgreSQL 19 (SQL/PGQ) — opens a series on the ISO SQL/PGQ syntax (CREATE PROPERTY GRAPH, GRAPH_TABLE) shipping in PG19, with worked examples over ordinary relational tables. (Hans-Jürgen Schönig · Cybertec)
  • When is a function leakproof? — Laurenz Albe digs into what LEAKPROOF really means for row-level security and security-barrier views, why it matters for RLS performance, and where the current definition is arguably too strict; framed as a request for comments. (Cybertec) [by committer]
  • pg_stat_statements: what it tells you, and what it can't — Radim Marek's two-part deep dive on why the same query splinters into many rows, how the fixed-size hash table evicts your tail under load, and why it counts rather than records — i.e. why it is not the query store you might want. (boringsql.com)
  • How the other half counts — Christophe Pettus contrasts how Postgres gathers planner statistics with the approaches taken by Oracle, Db2, MySQL, SQLite, DuckDB, and Snowflake. (thebuild.com)
  • data_checksums: a thirteen-year history still being written — uses the read-only data_checksums GUC as a hook into the long saga of page checksums, now finally toggleable online in PG19. (Christophe Pettus · thebuild.com)
  • Managed Postgres, examined: Azure Flexible Server — Azure's managed Postgres puts the standby in the commit path, so every write waits on synchronous replication to a second server — a real durability/latency trade-off competitors don't all make. (Christophe Pettus · thebuild.com)
  • A reviewer was born — Lætitia Avrot on the chronic shortage of patch reviewers: a clean, well-motivated io_uring fd-leak fix sat in the commitfest two months with zero reviewers, which is the norm, not the exception. (mydbanotebook.org)
  • Does pgBackRest work with pg_tde? — Stefan Fercot tests Percona's Transparent Data Encryption extension against pgBackRest and finds pgBackRest handles encrypted WAL segments transparently, contradicting an earlier claim that async archiving isn't supported. (pgstef.github.io)
  • pgsql_tweaks 1.0.3 released — Stefanie Janine Stölting's function/view bundle gets a maintenance release. (pgsql-tweaks.org)
    • tested against PostgreSQL 19 Beta 1
    • drops PostgreSQL 13 (EOL November 2025) from supported versions
    • get_markdown_doku_by_schema now uses COALESCE to avoid returning NULL on a NULL schema description

Migration experience

  • The night our tables wouldn't stop growing — a logical-replication migration where a 1-minute statement_timeout on the publisher kept killing the initial COPY; each restart left dead tuples behind, ballooning 50–90 GB source tables to 400+ GB on the subscriber overnight. Fix: ALTER ROLE replication_user SET statement_timeout = 0. (Semab Tariq · Stormatics)
  • File descriptors: the OS limit that takes down PostgreSQL — how fs.file-max and per-process RLIMIT_NOFILE exhaustion under high connection counts surfaces as a "database problem" that's really one layer down in the kernel, with the log sequence to recognize it. (Warda Bibi · Stormatics)

Research & cutting edge

  • GraftDB: dynamic folding of concurrent analytical queries — a multi-query execution engine that "grafts" a later-arriving query into a running one via shared operator state and per-query state lenses; TPC-H-derived workloads show up to 2.17x throughput and P95 latency as low as 0.17x an isolated-execution baseline. (arXiv) [paper]
  • LAANN: I/O-aware look-ahead search for disk-based ANN — a look-ahead search strategy for disk-resident approximate-nearest-neighbor indexes that overlaps and reorders I/O to cut latency on billion-scale vector search. (arXiv) [paper]

15 items · sources scanned: ~30 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~12

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-31 (week of 2026-05-25–2026-05-31)
item.content:encoded:
PostgreSQL

  • How soon is now()? A frozen-timestamp retry-loop bug — Oskar Dudycz walks through a real distributed-locking bug: now() returns the transaction start time, so a retry loop sharing one transaction kept re-evaluating last_updated < now() - timeout against a constant and never made progress; fix is clock_timestamp(). Includes the testing-seam lesson on why unit + e2e tests both missed it. (event-driven.io)
  • 5 PostgreSQL locking behaviours that trip people up — Shinya Kato (PostgreSQL engineer, NTT) on counterintuitive production-outage locks: FIFO lock-queue chained blocking behind a waiting ACCESS EXCLUSIVE, invisible FK FOR KEY SHARE deadlocks, unique-check INSERT deadlocks, the wraparound-prevention autovacuum that won't be cancelled, and VACUUM's hidden truncate-phase ACCESS EXCLUSIVE (and how it forcibly cancels standby SELECTs). (dev.to)
  • PostgreSQL Anonymizer 3.1 — Local Differential Privacy + critical CVE — Dalibo adds a formally-bounded LDP masking mechanism (Generalized Randomized Response, epsilon-tunable) for survey/categorical data, and fixes CVE-2026-9617, a privilege-escalation-to-superuser flaw (high risk on PG 14 and clusters upgraded from ≤14). Upgrade urged; SQL workaround posted. (Dalibo · postgresql.org)

PostgreSQL conferences (PGConf.dev 2026 trip reports)

  • PGConf.dev 2026: HA cluster-topology, wait-events, collation — Jeremy Schneider's trip summary surfaces real design directions discussed in Vancouver: Postgres lacks a first-class "cluster/topology" concept (a pg_nodes-style view, promotion-candidate discovery on standbys); Sami Imseih's pg_stat_statements LWLock/query-text-file concurrency patches; per-wait-event counters; and the unsolved ICU-upgrade-invalidates-indexes collation problem (with Oracle's pragmatic per-operator default as a reference). (ardentperf.com)
  • PGConf.dev 2026 reflections — SQL/PGQ, multithreading, resize-shared-buffers — Ashutosh Bapat reports on the graph-DB developer meeting (SQL/PGQ + AGE), the logical-replication working group (DDL replication and gaps before LR is usable for high-volume OLAP), the multithreading effort, and a quick design agreement with Databricks engineers (Heikki et al.) on resizing shared buffers without restart, built on the PG 19 shared-memory rework. (ashutoshpg.blogspot.com)

Migration experience

  • Crunchy Data PGO → Percona PostgreSQL Operator: two migration paths — Slava Sarzhan documents real cross-operator migrations on Kubernetes, with the actual mechanics rather than a pitch. (Slava Sarzhan · Percona) [vendor blog — substantive]
    • Standby-cluster method: percona.com
    • Backup-restore & PV-reuse method: percona.com

Releases

  • Percona Operator for PostgreSQL 3.0.0 — completes the hard fork from Crunchy PGO. (Percona)
    • inherited CRDs renamed under upstream.pgv2.percona.com, so the Crunchy and Percona operators can now coexist in the same cluster/namespace (no CRD/webhook/finalizer collision); one-time auto-migration on upgrade
    • fixed OpenShift OLM namespace scoping (watch list now honours the OperatorGroup)
    • major-version upgrades now use the official percona-distribution-postgresql-upgrade image so pg_upgrade runs the same signed binaries as production; note: pgaudit must be dropped/recreated manually and collation versions refreshed after PG 18 upgrade
    • drops CRD support for operator 2.7.0; built with Go 1.26; supports PostgreSQL 14–18
  • pg_statviz 1.1 — minimalist extension + utility for time-series capture and visualisation of internal PostgreSQL statistics (featured in Postgres Weekly #650). (github.com/vyruss)

New sources added this week

  • event-driven.io (Oskar Dudycz) — independent engineering blog with reproducible, first-hand Postgres bug stories (locking, time functions, logical replication, outbox patterns) tied to real production incidents. (event-driven.io)

8 items · sources scanned: ~25 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~12

item.title: DBMS Weekly — 2026-05-24 (week of 2026-05-18–2026-05-24)
item.content:encoded:
PostgreSQL

  • The four PG19 features you'll actually feel in production — Christophe Pettus skips the SQL/PGQ graph headline and flags the operational changes: 64-bit MultiXact members (kills the "vacuum-or-die" member-exhaustion failure mode), parallel autovacuum index workers (watch the max_workers × max_parallel × maintenance_work_mem worst case), temporal UPDATE/DELETE ... FOR PORTION OF, and jit = off by default (OLAP shops should re-enable and benchmark before upgrading). (thebuild.com)
  • pg_sorted_heap 0.14.0 — physically-sorted heap storage with zone-map pruning and a planner-integrated vector-search path. (postgresql.org)
    • sorted_hnsw made planner-visible via the Index AM path for covered KNN, including parent Merge Append over leaf HNSW indexes
    • composite zone-map metadata (first-key block ranges + bounded second-key summary); sorted_heap_bulk_load_ordered(...) trusted ordered ingestion
    • verified on PostgreSQL 16, 17, 18; now on PGXN
  • pg_tre 1.1.1 — approximate-regex index access method — a real IndexAmRoutine (USING tre) doing Levenshtein-distance regex matching ("within k edits of this pattern"), with WAL coverage, VACUUM awareness, and REINDEX CONCURRENTLY; three-tier filter funnel (BRIN-style range bloom → trigram postings → per-tuple bloom) over Laurikari's TRE library. Fills a gap pg_trgm/FTS/pgvector don't cover. PostgreSQL 18+. (postgresql.org)
  • pg_mentat 1.3.0 — Datomic-compatible Datalog inside Postgres — immutable datoms, a Datalog compiler that lowers to ordinary SQL, the pull API, time-travel (as-of/since/history), and ACID via PG transactions; built with pgrx in Rust for PG13–18, with soft integrations into pg_trgm/fuzzystrmatch/pgvector/PostGIS/etc. (postgresql.org)
  • plpgsql_wrap v1.0 — Oracle-style PL/pgSQL obfuscation — stored procedures written LANGUAGE plpgsql_wrap are AES-256-GCM-encrypted into pg_proc.prosrc; source is unreadable (including in pg_dump, whose output stays restorable) without the key, otherwise behaves identically to plpgsql. PostgreSQL 12+. (postgresql.org)
  • pg_statviz 1.0 — minimalist in-database stats time-series extension, now with an opt-in --ai analysis flag. (postgresql.org)
    • --ai sends each chart's data/PNG to a vision LLM (Claude/Gemini/local Ollama) for a HEALTHY/WARNING/CRITICAL verdict plus remediation
    • notable design discipline: a deterministic rules engine runs first and a "severity floor" prevents the LLM downgrading a real finding; prompts wrap user data in <user_data> envelopes for prompt-injection containment and explicitly debunk shared_buffers/random_page_cost folklore
    • AI is fully optional; with no --ai flag there are zero extra dependencies and no external calls
  • check_pgactivity 2.10 — new release of the Nagios/monitoring plugin for PostgreSQL. (postgresql.org)

Migration experience

  • 21 CFR Part 11 audit logging in Postgres without triggers — Nathan Leung walks through why Harbor rejected pgAudit (no app context) and PL/pgSQL triggers (SET LOCAL plumbing, per-study-DB duplication, PgBouncer transaction-pooling wariness) and instead wrapped their Drizzle ORM in a JS Proxy that forces .withAuditLogCtx(...) before any mutation, writing the data change and a signed before/after audit row in one transaction (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE for a consistent snapshot). Honest trade-offs section: extra round trips, weaker guarantee than a DB-level trigger, and a note on eventually moving signing into pgcrypto. (Nathan Leung · runharbor.com)

8 items · sources scanned: ~25 · filtered out as marketing/ads: ~9
